title: Rechargeable product with on board disposable battery charging
abstract: A rechargeable electric device is provided that includes each of rechargeable batteries and disposable batteries. The rechargeable batteries are embedded within the device, and the disposable batteries are stored within an easily accessible compartment of the device. The disposable batteries may be used to either charge the rechargeable batteries or to run the device as a reserve battery option. The device more efficiently uses the disposable batteries by charging the rechargeable batteries when the device is turned off. The device includes an indicator to monitor the power level of the rechargeable and/or disposable batteries. The device can be powered by the rechargeable batteries until they are depleted before automatically transitioning to the disposable batteries as a reserve power supply. The rechargeable and disposable batteries may also be used as a "power pack" for charging other devices such as cell phones.
claims:
Claims 1. A rechargeable device, the rechargeable device comprising: a housing; an electrical component contained within the housing; at least one rechargeable battery within the housing and in electrical communication with the electrical component to provide electrical power to the electrical component; at least one disposable battery within the housing and in electrical communication with the at least one rechargeable battery to provide electrical power to the at least one rechargeable batter; a controller in electrical communication with at least one of the at least one rechargeable battery and the at least one disposable battery to control delivery of electrical power therefrom; wherein when the rechargeable device is powered on, the at least one rechargeable battery powers the rechargeable device; and wherein when the rechargeable device is powered off, the at least one disposable battery recharges the at least one rechargeable battery. 2. The rechargeable device of claim 1 wherein the rechargeable device is a flashlight. 3. The rechargeable device of claim 1 wherein the rechargeable device includes at least one of an indicator light and a meter that indicates to a user the power level of at least one of the at least one rechargeable battery and the at least one disposable battery. 4. The rechargeable device of claim 1 wherein the at least one rechargeable battery is housed in an enclosed compartment. 5. The rechargeable device of claim 1 wherein the rechargeable device includes a micro USB port used to at least one of power the rechargeable device and charge the at least one rechargeable battery or the at least one disposable battery. 6. The rechargeable device of claim 1 wherein the at least one rechargeable battery is at least one of a lead-acid, NiCd, NiMH, Li-ion, and Li-ion polymer battery. 7. The rechargeable device of claim 1 wherein the at least one disposable battery is at least one of an alkaline size A, AA, C and D battery. 8. A power pack device, the power pack device comprising: at least one rechargeable battery housed within the power pack device; at least one disposable battery housed within the power pack device; an output port for electronic communication with a powered device; wherein when a powered device is connected to the power pack device via the output port, the at least one rechargeable battery is used to charge the powered device; and wherein when a powered device is disconnected from the power pack device, the at least one disposable battery is used to recharge the at least one rechargeable battery. 9. The power pack device of claim 8 wherein the output port is a USB port. 10. The power pack device of claim 8 wherein the power pack device includes at least one of an indicator light and a meter that indicates to a user the power level of at least one of the at least one rechargeable battery and the at least one disposable battery. 11. The power pack device of claim 1 wherein the at least one rechargeable battery housed in an enclosed compartment. 12. The power pack device of claim 1 wherein the at least one rechargeable battery is at least one of a lead-acid, NiCd, NiMH, Li-ion, and Li-ion polymer battery. 13. The power pack device of claim 1 wherein the at least one disposable battery is at least one of an alkaline size A, AA, C and D battery. 14. A method for powering an electronic device, the method comprising the steps of: 

 powering the electronic device via at least one rechargeable battery when the electronic device is powered on; and recharging the at least one rechargeable battery via at least one disposable battery when the electronic devise is powered off. 15. The method of claim 14, the method further comprising the step of: indicating to a user the battery strength of at least one of the at least one rechargeable battery and the at least one disposable battery.
